html:
The Yellow Gem is an item used to craft Ancient items. It is required in two main crafting recipes; the Magiluminescence and Star Caller's Staff. The Magiluminescence is an amulet that emits light, increases movement speed, and provides a Sanity boost when worn. The Star Caller's Staff summons a Dwarf Star that emits light and heat. It can also be used for Cooking Food.

Yellow Gems can be inserted into Cratered Moonrocks to acquire a Yellow Moonlens. They can also be used to power Winona's G.E.M.erator.

Gathering

Yellow Gems can be obtained by mining Ancient Statues, destroying Broken Clockworks, opening Ornate Chests in the Ruins. They can also be gathered from Cave Holes by using The Lazy Forager. The Ruins can be regenerated by killing the Ancient Fuelweaver, allowing the player to obtain new gems each time the boss is defeated.

There are additional ways to acquire Yellow Gems:

  • The Dragonfly drops 1–2 Yellow Gems upon death.
  • Retinazor can be killed every 15 days for a guaranteed Yellow Gem.
  • Boulderboughs planted in the Ruins or the Fumarole Biome have a chance to grow Yellow Gems.
  • Geode Fruits have roughly a 2.4% chance to yield a Yellow Gem when heated.
  • The Large Ornate Chest has a chance to contain Yellow Gems each time the Ancient Guardian is killed.
  • Sunken Chests have a chance to drop Yellow Gems when hammered.
  • The Malbatross has a 5% chance to drop a single Yellow Gem upon death.
  • The Table and Chair Relics have roughly a 0.2% chance to drop Yellow Gems when destroyed.
  • Tumbleweeds have roughly a 0.01% chance to drop Yellow Gems when harvested.
  • Each segment of a defeated Great Depths Worm has a chance to drop a Yellow Gem.
  • A Yellow Gem can be crafted by Wilson using the Transmute Gems II skill.
  • Yellow Gems can be obtained from presents under a Winter's Feast Tree (only during Winter's Feast).

Don't Starve Hungry For Your Hunger None Ewecus has been added to the game.
Hamlet The Aporkalypse! 298452 Fixed a bug due to which the yellow gem became invisible when placed on a shelf.

–Wilson

–Willow

–Wolfgang

–Wheeler
“I like gems best before they're cut.”
–Winona

–Wortox
“There sunshine inside it.”
–Wurt
“Is it supposed to feel warm?”
–Walter
“It won't do much good by itself.”
–Wanda
The Yellow Gem is an item used to craft Ancient items. It is required in two main crafting recipes; the Magiluminescence and Star Caller's Staff. The Magiluminescence is an amulet that emits light, increases movement speed, and provides a Sanity boost when worn. The Star Caller's Staff summons a Dwarf Star that emits light and heat. It can also be used for Cooking Food.
Yellow Gems can be inserted into Cratered Moonrocks to acquire a Yellow Moonlens. They can also be used to power Winona's G.E.M.erator.
Gathering[edit | edit source]
Yellow Gems can be obtained by mining Ancient Statues, destroying Broken Clockworks, opening Ornate Chests in the Ruins. They can also be gathered from Cave Holes by using The Lazy Forager. The Ruins can be regenerated by killing the Ancient Fuelweaver, allowing the player to obtain new gems each time the boss is defeated.
There are additional ways to acquire Yellow Gems:
The Dragonfly drops 1–2 Yellow Gems upon death.
Retinazor can be killed every 15 days for a guaranteed Yellow Gem.
Boulderboughs planted in the Ruins or the Fumarole Biome have a chance to grow Yellow Gems.
Geode Fruits have roughly a 2.4% chance to yield a Yellow Gem when heated.
The Large Ornate Chest has a chance to contain Yellow Gems each time the Ancient Guardian is killed.
Sunken Chests have a chance to drop Yellow Gems when hammered.
The Malbatross has a 5% chance to drop a single Yellow Gem upon death.
The Table and Chair Relics have roughly a 0.2% chance to drop Yellow Gems when destroyed.
Tumbleweeds have roughly a 0.01% chance to drop Yellow Gems when harvested.
Each segment of a defeated Great Depths Worm has a chance to drop a Yellow Gem.
A Yellow Gem can be crafted by Wilson using the Transmute Gems II skill.
Yellow Gems can be obtained from presents under a Winter's Feast Tree (only during Winter's Feast).
